title: Edappadi K Palaniswami and AIADMK's New Era in Tamil Nadu
content:
For the men and women standing under the shade of Ashoka trees, the memory of MGR forming the AIADMK party in 1972 remains vividly alive. Today, they form the core of a crowd gathered to see Edappadi K Palaniswami, widely known as EPS, arrive in a bulletproof recreational vehicle. As his smiling face emerges from the sun-roof, a hype-man screams into a megaphone, whipping the crowd into a frenzy. EPS uses the opportunity to draw a sharp contrast between his own rise and the dynasty politics of his main rival, Chief Minister MK Stalin of the DMK. He reminds the crowd that while Stalin had an easy path as the son of a prominent leader, he had to struggle his way up from the very bottom.

The crowd roars in approval as EPS proudly highlights his humble beginnings as an ordinary party worker. He challenges the audience to name anyone from the DMK, apart from family members, who could rise to such heights and share the stage with the leader. This rhetorical question resonates deeply with the gathered supporters, as they already know the answer. EPS's dramatic rise to power defines the modern era of the AIADMK, a party still operating in the sheltering shadow of its great matriarch, J Jayalalithaa. His journey from a self-confessed nobody to the general secretary and chief minister is a story of loyalty, intrigue, and relentless ambition.

Following the death of Jayalalithaa in the winter of 2016, the party was thrust into a period of intense instability and internal conflict. In this churn of alliances and betrayals, men rose and fell as they fought for control of the massive political machine she left behind. It was out of this chaos that EPS eventually emerged victorious, securing the helm of the party. As he attacks his rivals to eat into their anti-incumbency vote share, his narrative of common-man struggle has become his sharpest political weapon. Under his leadership, the AIADMK continues to navigate the complex fault lines of Tamil Nadu politics.
